# ggaction

ggaction is a JavaScript chart-authoring library built around immutable,
hierarchical actions. This file points to canonical documentation for humans
and language models.

## Start

- Documentation index: ./index.md
- Getting started: ./getting-started.md
- Complete scatterplot tutorial: ./tutorials/scatterplot.md
- Complete line chart tutorial: ./tutorials/line-chart.md
- Complete action reference: ./reference/actions.md
- Supported features: ./supported-features.md

## Core concepts

- ChartProgram and immutability: ./concepts/chart-program.md
- Semantic and graphical state: ./concepts/semantic-and-graphics.md
- Actions and trace trees: ./concepts/actions-and-trace.md

## API

- Canvas: ./api/canvas.md
- Data: ./api/data.md
- Marks: ./api/marks.md
- Encodings: ./api/encodings.md
- Position encodings: ./api/position-encodings.md
- Series encodings: ./api/series-encodings.md
- Constant appearance: ./api/appearance.md
- Scale options: ./api/scales.md
- Coordinates: ./api/coordinates.md
- Guides: ./api/guides.md
- Axes: ./api/axes.md
- Legends: ./api/legends.md
- Titles: ./api/titles.md
- Rendering: ./api/rendering.md
- Advanced axis components: ./advanced/axis-components.md
- Action authoring: ./extension/action-authoring.md
- Primitive extension API: ./extension/primitives.md

## Architectural invariants

- Every action accepts one plain option object and returns a new ChartProgram.
- Existing programs and caller-owned inputs are never mutated.
- semanticSpec records chart meaning.
- graphicSpec stores fully materialized backend-neutral graphics.
- There is no automatic semantic-to-graphic compiler.
- Domain actions explicitly invoke every required graphical materialization.
- Renderers read graphicSpec only; they do not infer from semanticSpec.
- Nested wrapped actions form the hierarchical trace.
- Positional encodings create and attach coordinates; guide actions only read them.
- encodeRadius is fixed circle appearance, not a Polar radial encoding.
- Temporal line encodeX resolves a time scale but does not yet materialize path points.
- Aggregate line encodeY computes mean groups and explicitly materializes sorted paths.
- Line encodeColor and encodeStrokeDash regroup and style concrete series paths.
- createBarMark stores a semantic bar and creates an empty rect collection.
- Binned bar encodeX stores maxBins, resolves a nice linear x domain/range, and deliberately leaves rect materialization for later y encoding.
- Bar encodeY infers the binned x field, stores count/zero-stack, resolves both scales, and materializes concrete rects for non-empty bins.
- encodeHistogram is a thin aggregate whose direct children are binned bar encodeX and count/zero-stack encodeY.
- createAxes supports linear and UTC time scales plus aggregate-aware title inference.
- createLegend combines compatible line color and strokeDash encodings into one concrete series legend.
- createTitle stores semantic title text and materializes a plot-aligned top title with an optional subtitle.
- createGuides calls the applicable createAxes and createLegend wrapped actions; it never creates chart titles.

## Current scope

The documented build is version 0.0.0-dev and is not published to npm. Use the
repository source directly.

The implemented end-to-end Chart API covers Cartesian point scatterplots and
temporal mean-aggregate line charts with browser Canvas and PNG output.
`createBarMark` creates an empty rect collection, and binned bar `encodeX`
stores the horizontal histogram meaning plus a resolved x scale. Bar
`encodeY()` infers count/zero-stack and resolves the y scale from total bin
counts, then materializes concrete uncolored histogram rects. Public bar color
encoding and automatic histogram legend generation are not yet implemented.
The equivalent `encodeHistogram` action composes those x/y actions without
duplicating their validation, inference, or materialization behavior.
Temporal line `encodeX` resolves a time scale; aggregate line `encodeY`
materializes sorted series paths. Line `encodeColor` and `encodeStrokeDash`
group and style series through ordinal scales. `createGuides` composes time or
linear axes with the compatible right-side line-series legend. A plot-aligned
top title and optional subtitle are available separately through `createTitle`.
Do not assume roadmap features are implemented.
